pr.title: mncharsel switch case enum, improve naming of some associated functions
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: This PR replaced hard-coded character-select match_type switch values in mncharsel with a new CSSMatchType enum, renamed an anonymous inline time-formatting helper to drawTimeText with descriptive parameter names, and promoted several gm_1601 high-score accessors from address-based names to semantic names for Multiman/Stadium records. The changes were documentation/readability-oriented and did not appear to alter behavior.
what_changed: Added typedef enum CSSMatchType in src/melee/mn/types.h, mapping match_type values 0x0 through 0x16 to names such as VS_MELEE, REG_CLASSIC, STADIUM_TARGET, STADIUM_3_MIN_MELEE, STADIUM_ENDLESS_MELEE, and STADIUM_CRUEL_MELEE.
Updated mnCharSel_8025C020 switch cases from numeric literals 11 through 22 to CSSMatchType enum constants, clarifying which regular/stadium mode each branch handles.
Renamed static inline helper inline1 to drawTimeText and changed generic parameter names r6/r7/r8/r31 to hours/minutes/seconds/microseconds.

Renamed gm_80163DC4, gm_80163DE8, gm_80163E3C, gm_80163E60, gm_80163EB4, gm_80163ED8, gm_80163F2C, and gm_80163F50 to high-score getter names for 3-Minute Multiman, 15-Minute Multiman, Endless, and Cruel modes.
Updated gm_1601.h declarations and config/GALE01/symbols.txt entries to keep public prototypes and symbol names synchronized with the renamed functions.
Updated mncharsel call sites to use the new high-score getter names when rendering KO totals for STADIUM_3_MIN_MELEE, STADIUM_15_MIN_MELEE, STADIUM_ENDLESS_MELEE, and STADIUM_CRUEL_MELEE.
smart_moves: Introduced the enum in the shared mn types header rather than leaving mode IDs local to one switch, making future match_type uses easier to identify and less error-prone.
Kept the enum values explicit in hexadecimal, preserving the exact underlying ABI/data values while improving readability.
Renamed both individual-score and total-score functions in matched pairs, making the gm_1601 API easier to scan and reducing ambiguity between per-character high scores and aggregate totals.
Renamed the time display helper based on observable behavior and format strings instead of leaving it as inline1; parameter names now explain the clamping/formatting logic.
Updated source, header declarations, and symbols.txt together, avoiding stale declarations or symbol-map drift after address-to-semantic renames.
decomp_lessons: When a switch over a known mode field contains many magic numbers, introduce an enum with exact numeric assignments before attempting deeper control-flow or struct work.
Address-named functions can often be safely promoted when their call sites and underlying accessors reveal a stable pattern, such as per-mode high-score retrieval and total aggregation.
Small helper renames are valuable when format strings reveal semantics; here "%02d:%02d", "%d:%02d:%02d", and fallback "99:59:59" supported naming the helper as a time text renderer.
For decomp readability patches, synchronize names across implementation, header, call sites, and config symbol files in the same PR.
Enum constants for game/menu modes should describe user-facing modes, not just storage values, because they make UI branch logic much easier to review.
naming_conventions: CSS match-type enum is named CSSMatchType.
Versus modes use VS_* names, including VS_MELEE, VS_CAMERA, VS_STAMINA, VS_SUDDEN_DEATH, VS_GIANT, VS_TINY, VS_INVISIBLE, VS_FIXED_CAMERA, VS_SINGLE_BUTTON, VS_LIGHTNING, and VS_SLOWMO.
Regular one-player modes use REG_* names: REG_CLASSIC, REG_ADVENTURE, REG_ALLSTAR.
Stadium modes use STADIUM_* names: STADIUM_TARGET, STADIUM_HOMERUN, STADIUM_MULTIMAN_10, STADIUM_MULTIMAN_100, STADIUM_3_MIN_MELEE, STADIUM_15_MIN_MELEE, STADIUM_ENDLESS_MELEE, STADIUM_CRUEL_MELEE.

Game-mode high-score accessors use gm_Get<Mode>Highscore for per-character values and gm_Get<Mode>TotalHighscore for totals, for example gm_Get3MinMultimanHighscore and gm_Get3MinMultimanTotalHighscore.
Local static inline helpers can use lowerCamelCase descriptive names such as drawTimeText when they are not exported symbols.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No explicit matching discussion was present in the PR context.
The patch appears behavior-preserving: magic constants were replaced by enum names with identical values, and function renames retained addresses, prototypes, and call structure.
Explicit enum values help avoid codegen or data interpretation surprises by preserving the original numeric match_type values.
Renaming inline1 to drawTimeText retained its static inline form, which likely avoided disturbing inlining behavior while improving source clarity.
review_feedback: No review comments were included in the provided excerpt.
The PR had one issue comment according to the metadata, but its body was not available in the supplied context, so no reviewer intent can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Find other uses of CSSData.match_type that still compare against numeric literals and convert them to CSSMatchType.
Search for remaining gm_80163* functions in gm_1601 that may have high-score or stadium semantics suitable for naming.
Audit mncharsel helpers still named inline* or with register-style parameters for format-string-supported renames.
Check whether CSSData.match_type should be typed as CSSMatchType instead of u8, if doing so does not affect matching or layout.
